name: Chicken Soup w/ Apples & Leeks
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the chicken with salt and pepper.
2. Melt the butter over medium heat in a saute pan large enough to hold the chicken.
3. Add the chicken, skin side down, and cook for about eight minutes over medium heat. If the butter starts to brown, lower the heat slightly.
4. Turn the chicken and lightly saute it for five more minutes.
5. Remove the chicken and place it in a bowl. If the butter has burned, pour it out and replace with two tablespoons of fresh butter.
6. Add the leeks and cook them over medium heat for 10 minutes, stirring every couple of minutes until they are soft but not brown.
7. Return the chicken to the pan with the leeks.
8. Add the apples, apple juice, vinegar, and broth. Bring the liquids to a slow simmer. Skim off any fat or froth that floats to the top with a ladle.
9. Cover the pot and gently simmer the chicken for 10 to 15 minutes until fully cooked.
10. Remove the chicken and let it cool. Discard the skin and pull the meat away from the bones, cutting into half-inch chunks.
11. Skim off any fat that has formed on the surface of the liquid using a ladle.
12. Add the Calvados and heavy cream. Bring to a simmer and season with salt and pepper to taste.
13. Heat the chicken pieces in the soup for two minutes and serve immediately in hot bowls.
Suggestions and variations:
Try adding sliced mushrooms along with the apples. The mushroom flavor complements the other ingredients, making the soup more substantial. This soup is also delicious with curry; gently cook a tablespoon of curry powder in butter before stirring into the soup shortly before serving.
